name: Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa
name_original: Barrancos de Valcongosto y Cuartún — circular desde Purujosa
region_intro: Hiking around Purujosa offers access to trails within the Moncayo Natural Park, characterized by its mountainous landscape, deep gorges, and diverse forests. The region features a mix of rocky outcrops, river valleys, and elevated viewpoints. The terrain provides varied conditions for hikers, from moderate ascents to more challenging climbs. Best hiking trails around Purujosa The most popular hiking route is Peñas de Herrera - circular from Talamantes, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding hills and valleys. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This trail navigates through varied terrain, including ascents to Cerro Morrón. Local hikers also love the Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through Valcongosto Gorges and the Cuartún area, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Purujosa is defined by mountainous terrain, deep gorges, and forested paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Purujosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Purujosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Purujosa?
A: Purujosa offers access to over 30 hiking trails within the Moncayo Natural Park. These routes vary in difficulty, providing options for different experience levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are a few easy trails around Purujosa, with 2 specifically categorized as easy and 24 as moderate. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, many moderate trails can be suitable for families with some hiking experience. For a moderate option that is popular and offers scenic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971965058/valcongosto-gorges-and-cuartun-circular-from-purujosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 miles (7.3 km) long.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find in the Purujosa area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Purujosa offers 7 difficult trails. A notable challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971994351/cueva-de-los-pilares-and-cerro-morron-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 9.7-mile (15.7 km) path features significant ascents to Cerro Morrón and varied terrain, making it a demanding option.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Purujosa?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971965058/valcongosto-gorges-and-cuartun-circular-from-purujosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through scenic gorges and the Cuartún area.

Q: What are some interesting natural landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt;, a fascinating cave formation. For panoramic views, consider trails that lead to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth seeking out on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain around Purujosa offers several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; are popular spots for expansive vistas of the surrounding hills and valleys. Some trails also lead to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Morrón Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering elevated perspectives.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails around Purujosa?
A: The trails around Purujosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from deep gorges to forested paths, and the opportunities for both moderate walks and challenging ascents within the Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: Is Purujosa a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails within the Moncayo Natural Park can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Purujosa area, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb wildlife or other hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Purujosa?
A: The best time to hike in Purujosa is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters may bring snow to higher parts of the Moncayo Natural Park, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads in Purujosa?
A: Parking availability can vary depending on the specific trailhead. In Purujosa itself, you can usually find parking within the village. For trails starting outside the village, it's common to find designated parking areas or roadside spots near popular access points. Always check maps or route descriptions for specific parking recommendations for your chosen hike.

Q: What should I pack for a hike in Purujosa?
A: Given the mountainous and varied terrain, it's recommended to pack sturdy hiking boots, layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot), sun protection, and a first-aid kit. For longer or more challenging routes, trekking poles can also be beneficial.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails around Purujosa using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly to all trailheads around Purujosa may be limited, as it is a more rural area. It's generally easier to access the region by car. However, some local bus services might connect Purujosa to larger towns, from which you could potentially arrange further transport or start a hike if the trailhead is accessible from the village.
tips: The so-called "strait of Gibraltar" is located in the Valcongosto ravine, very close to the town of Purujosa. It is advisable to walk with firm footing since the terrain is usually slippery depending on the water level, but it is definitely worth it.

Before going down the Cuartún ravine and passing through the homonymous cave, it is worth stopping to observe the beautiful and imposing Barrevinoso mole. If we go in the direction of Purujosa, to our left we can also see the Muela de Cuartún.
We are in the Cuartún ravine and a few meters after entering it, we will see a path to the left going down towards Purujosa to see the beautiful Cuartún cave, which was formerly used to guard cattle.

waypoints.name: View of La Muela de Barrevinoso
